bullets: 29 production evaluation batches from JD.com covering multi-warehouse inventory allocation in large-scale e-commerce, distributing fixed replenishment quantities across warehouses under heterogeneous demand and coverage constraints.

An unnamed LLM generates candidate operations-research formulations and executable solver code from semi-structured natural-language specs, while a solver verifies executability, feasibility, and quality; the specific model is not stated.
The best single formulation raised allocation accuracy 3.4 percentage points over the incumbent, while the full ORLA framework gained 4.5 points and improved allocation in 26 of 29 batches.

Over 55,000 forecast-rationale pairs from a multiyear forecasting tournament, with probabilistic judgments and written rationales scored against realized outcomes at both forecast and forecaster levels.

Unnamed large language models score sixty theory-guided reasoning patterns, called Explanation Quality Markers, in each rationale; benchmarked against pre-LLM text methods and human ratings, with no model named.

EQMs predict accuracy and outperform pre-LLM methods, matching directional hypotheses in over 90 percent of significant correlations, and identify likely underperformers more reliably than the very best forecasters.
321 spreadsheet tasks built from authentic financial reports and corporate filings, each instance averaging 11.8 worksheets and 593.5 cell modifications, annotated and validated by domain experts.

Eight frontier large language models, not named, run under a unified multi-turn agent scaffold on generation, debugging, and visualization tasks, with outputs scored against expert ground-truth solutions.
The best model reached 34.89 percent overall task accuracy and debugging accuracy fell to 12 percent; insufficient spreadsheet inspection and wrong target-cell selection were the dominant failures.
A conceptual design essay on the use of AI in qualitative research, with no empirical sample, period, geography, or dataset reported.
Discusses large language models generally, with no model named, for coding and categorization, and sets out three design criteria implemented in the Skimle.com tool rather than testing any model.
Proposes predictable processes, two-way transparency between data and conclusions, and researcher control over AI coding to augment rather than automate expert interpretive judgment.

A simulation benchmark whose synthetic market decouples observable price from hidden fundamental value, testing autonomous financial agents over long trading horizons across calm, crash, and bubble regimes.

Eighteen frontier and open-source large language models, none named, act as trading agents under three behavioral mandates, with their decisions treated as behavior to be studied rather than measurements.
Mandate salience decay compounds over time and is model-dependent; in crashes the behavioral gap grows 4.4 times by the final quarter, and re-grounding helps conservative but harms aggressive agents.
